name: data-table
role: Top-level scope for a data table surface. Polymorphic <div role='region' aria-label='<table-name> table'> containing optional toolbar, semantic <table>, optional empty-state, and optional pagination. Three densities (compact / default / comfortable), three visual registers (striped / bordered / minimal), three selection modes (none / single / multi). All seven pattern states (default / loading / empty / filtered-empty / error / selected / sorted) signal via [data-state] for CSS targeting; ARIA state on the inner <table> or <tr> elements per WAI-ARIA Grid + Table specs.

semanticStructure: <div role='region' aria-label='<table-name> table'> — wraps everything; the region landmark gives screen readers a clear handle for skip-to-content
<table> — true semantic <table> with <thead> / <tbody> / <tfoot>. NEVER use <div> grid for tabular data — WCAG 1.3.1 (Info & Relationships) requires real table semantics.
(optional) <data-table-empty-state> — only renders when state ∈ {empty, filtered-empty, error}

densityScale.compact: Cell padding --space-2 --space-3. Used in admin dashboards with high row count (50+ visible rows), audit logs, financial transaction lists.
densityScale.default: Cell padding --space-3 --space-card. The 80% choice — readable, scannable, not cramped.
densityScale.comfortable: Cell padding --space-4 --space-card. Used on settings pages, customer record lists, anywhere row count is low and each row has weight.

registers.striped: Alternating row backgrounds via tbody tr:nth-child(even) { background: --surface-recessed }. Used when row count is high and scanning across is the primary interaction.
registers.bordered: Full grid lines via border on every <td>. Used in financial / accounting contexts and anywhere precise column alignment matters.
registers.minimal: Row separators only (border-bottom on tbody tr). The canonical Linear / Notion / Stripe register. Default for most modern admin surfaces.

selectionModes.none: No selection checkboxes. Read-only table. The default.
selectionModes.single: Radio-style selection — choosing a row deselects all others. Used when a single row maps to a detail-view destination.
selectionModes.multi: Checkbox selection on each row + a master checkbox in the header. Selecting rows reveals <data-table-bulk-actions>. The most common selection mode for admin surfaces (bulk delete, bulk export, bulk assign).

ariaPatterns.regionLandmark: <div role='region' aria-label='<table-name> table'> — gives the table surface a named landmark for skip-to-content + AT navigation.
ariaPatterns.tableSemantics: Always use <table> for tabular data. Adding ARIA role='grid' is only appropriate when implementing keyboard-grid navigation (arrow keys move cell-to-cell, like a spreadsheet). For simple sortable/filterable tables, plain <table> + sort affordances on <th> is preferred — simpler keyboard model, better default AT support.
ariaPatterns.ariaRowcount: When virtualizing rows (only rendering visible window of a 10000-row dataset), set aria-rowcount on <table> and aria-rowindex on each visible <tr>. NOT required for non-virtualized tables.
ariaPatterns.captionVsAriaLabel: <table><caption>Customer records</caption> is preferred over aria-label when the caption text should be visible. Use aria-label only for landmarks where the caption would be redundant with a visible heading above the table.

name: data-table-toolbar
role: Toolbar row above the <table> hosting search input, filter chips/dropdowns, and (when rows are selected) bulk-action group. Three registers — search-only, search-plus-filter, full. Composes <form-control type='search'> from pattern-form-fields and <action-button> from pattern-button-system.

semanticStructure: <form-control type='search' placeholder='Search…'> — composes from form-fields with leading-slot magnifier icon and trailing-slot clear button
<div class='filter-cluster'> — filter chips / dropdowns; each filter is itself an <action-button> or <form-control type='select'>
<div role='group' aria-label='Bulk actions' hidden> — only visible when rows are selected; data-table-bulk-actions content

registers.search-only: Just the search input. Used on simple lookup tables.
registers.search-plus-filter: Search input + filter chip cluster (typically 2-4 chips for status / role / date range). The 80% solution.
registers.full: Search + filter chips + sort selector + density toggle + export button. Used on dense admin surfaces.

meta.composesFrom: @quoin/pattern-form-fields (search + select inputs) + @quoin/pattern-button-system (filter chip toggles, density toggle, export button)

ariaPatterns.toolbarRole: <div role='toolbar' aria-label='Table controls'> wraps the cluster. role='toolbar' tells AT this is a collection of related controls.
ariaPatterns.filterChipState: Active filter chips carry aria-pressed='true'. The chip's aria-label includes the filter dimension ('Filter: Status — Active').
ariaPatterns.searchSubmission: Search input fires on input (debounced) — no Submit button. aria-live='polite' announces row count change ('Showing 47 of 312 customers').

name: data-table-header
role: <thead><tr> row containing <th> elements per column. Sortable columns wrap their label in a <button> that toggles sort state and carries aria-sort. Non-sortable columns just contain plain text. Header is sticky-on-scroll by default within the table's scroll container.

semanticStructure: <thead> position-sticky to viewport top within the table scroll container
<button type='button'> — only inside sortable headers; the entire header label is the button's text

ariaPatterns.ariaSortValues: aria-sort='none' (default), 'ascending' (sorted A→Z / 1→9), 'descending' (sorted Z→A / 9→1). Exactly one column may have a non-'none' value at a time (single-column sort) or multiple may (composite sort — show numerical priority in the indicator).
ariaPatterns.scopeCol: Every <th> in <thead> gets scope='col'. This associates the header semantically with the column data below it.
ariaPatterns.sortKeyboardActivation: Sortable <th> contains a <button type='button'>; Enter and Space activate the sort. Tab moves between sortable columns; Tab DOES NOT skip non-sortable headers entirely (they're just not focusable themselves).
ariaPatterns.ariaLabelOnSortButton: The sort button's accessible name should include current state: '<Column>, sorted ascending. Activate to sort descending.' Update aria-label dynamically on sort change.

name: data-table-row
role: <tr> in <tbody>. Hosts <td> cells. Supports four states — default / hover / selected / disabled. With selection enabled, the first cell is a selection checkbox (multi) or radio (single). Multi-select rows respect Shift+Click for range selection. Optional row-level action affordance: rightmost cell with kebab menu or inline buttons.

semanticStructure: (if selectable) <td> — selection checkbox or radio composing form-fields
(optional) <td> — row action affordance (kebab menu, inline buttons)

selectionInteraction.click: Clicking the selection checkbox toggles that row's selection.
selectionInteraction.shiftClick: Shift+Click selects the range from the last-selected row through the clicked row.
selectionInteraction.masterCheckbox: Clicking the master checkbox in the header selects/deselects ALL visible rows. The master checkbox shows indeterminate state when some-but-not-all rows are selected.
selectionInteraction.keyboardNav: Selection checkbox is focusable via Tab; Space toggles.

ariaPatterns.ariaSelected: aria-selected on <tr> for multi-select. The selection checkbox is the activation surface; aria-selected is the state.
ariaPatterns.ariaDisabled: aria-disabled='true' on disabled rows. The row's checkbox is also disabled. Row is not focusable.
ariaPatterns.rowClickability: Whole-row click to navigate is OPTIONAL — when enabled, the <tr> needs role='link' OR the first cell's content is a link spanning the row. Don't make <tr> itself clickable without proper ARIA — that breaks AT row navigation.

name: data-table-cell
role: <td> with content-type-aware rendering. Six canonical content types — text (default), number (tabular-nums + right-align), status-badge (coloured pill for state values), avatar-stack (composed via vocab-app-shell), actions (button cluster), and link (anchor leading to detail view). Width is fluid; alignment depends on type.

contentTypes.text: Plain text. Default left-aligned. Truncates with ellipsis when truncate='true' and width exceeded.
contentTypes.number: Numeric values. font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums for column-aligned digits. Right-aligned by default (numbers compare best when right-aligned with consistent decimal positions).
contentTypes.status-badge: Pill-shaped badge in semantic colour. Tones: --success / --warning / --critical / --accent / --text-recede. Used for status enums (Active / Pending / Suspended / Archived).
contentTypes.avatar-stack: Composes <avatar-stack> from @quoin/vocab-app-shell. Used for 'Assigned to' or 'Members' columns. 3-5 avatars with +N overflow.
contentTypes.actions: Cluster of inline buttons or a kebab menu. Composes <action-button intent='ghost' size='sm'> from pattern-button-system. Typically rightmost column.
contentTypes.link: Anchor to row detail view. Underlined on hover; entire cell is the click target.

meta.truncation: When truncate='true' + the content overflows, the cell shows ellipsis + a tooltip on hover/focus revealing the full value. Tooltip is the browser's title attribute by default; can be promoted to a styled popover at the host page level.

ariaPatterns.scopeRow: First <td> in each row gets scope='row' if it's the row's identifying value (e.g. customer name) — gives AT a row-header to navigate by.
ariaPatterns.numberAriaLabel: Numeric cells with units should include the unit in an aria-label or hidden span: '$1,234.561234 dollars and 56 cents'.
ariaPatterns.statusBadgeText: Status badges must have readable text content — colour alone is not sufficient per WCAG 1.4.1.

name: data-table-empty-state
role: Empty / no-results / error message rendered inside <tbody><tr><td colspan='all'> when the table has no rows OR the filter yielded nothing. Three variants — empty (never had data, prompt to create), filtered-empty (filter yields nothing, prompt to clear), error (load failed, prompt to retry).

semanticStructure: No results / Nothing to show / Couldn't load
Helpful context message — what's missing and what to do next
<div class='actions'> — composing button-system: 'Create first record' / 'Clear filters' / 'Retry'

variants.empty: First-run / never-had-data. Heading: 'No <noun> yet'. Body: brief value-prop + primary CTA 'Create your first <noun>'.
variants.filtered-empty: Filter yielded nothing. Heading: 'No matches'. Body: '<n> <noun> total — try a different filter.' Secondary CTA 'Clear filters' (ghost intent).
variants.error: Load failed. Heading: 'Couldn't load <noun>'. Body: error context. Secondary CTA 'Retry' (secondary intent). Optional 'Contact support' link.

ariaPatterns.liveRegion: When the table transitions from default → filtered-empty (filter applied yields zero rows), the empty-state's heading should be inside an aria-live='polite' region so AT announces 'No matches'.
ariaPatterns.errorRoleAlert: For the error variant specifically, wrap the heading in role='alert' so AT announces immediately.

name: data-table-pagination
role: Bottom-anchored pagination controls. Hosts prev/next buttons, page-size selector, and 'Showing 1-20 of 312' count text. Renders as <nav aria-label='Pagination'>. Three registers — compact (just prev/next + count), default (prev/next + page numbers + count), extended (everything plus page-size selector + jump-to-page input).

ariaPatterns.ariaCurrentPage: The active page button gets aria-current='page'. Visible underline + emphasized colour.
ariaPatterns.countLiveRegion: The 'Showing X-Y of Z' span has aria-live='polite' so AT announces page changes.
ariaPatterns.disabledPrevNext: Prev button is aria-disabled on page 1; Next is aria-disabled on the final page. Don't remove them from DOM — keeping them present with disabled state keeps the layout stable.
ariaPatterns.keyboardModel: Tab through controls left-to-right. Enter / Space activate. Arrow keys NOT used (would conflict with horizontal scroll). Home/End jump to first/last page on the visible page-number cluster.

name: data-table-bulk-actions
role: Contextual action bar that appears (slides down from below the toolbar OR replaces the toolbar contents) when one or more rows are selected. Hosts bulk operations — delete, export, change-status, assign, archive. Composes <action-button> from pattern-button-system. ARIA: role='group' aria-label='Bulk actions for <n> selected rows'.

semanticStructure: <div role='group' aria-label='Bulk actions for <n> selected rows'>

registers.inline: Replaces the toolbar's filter cluster while rows are selected. Search remains visible.
registers.banner: Slides in as a separate row above the toolbar. Both filter cluster and bulk-actions are visible simultaneously.

ariaPatterns.groupRole: <div role='group' aria-label='Bulk actions for 3 selected rows'> — aria-label includes the count for context.
ariaPatterns.countLiveRegion: Selection count in aria-live='polite' so AT announces count changes.
ariaPatterns.destructiveConfirmation: Critical-intent bulk actions (Delete, Archive) must open a confirmation modal — they should NEVER execute on the first click. This is delegated to pattern-modal-dialog.
